name_en: St. Moritz Dorf Old Village Center

phrases.intent_en: Where can I buy ski passes nearby
phrases.text_local: Wo gibt es Skipässe zu kaufen?
phrases.cultural_note: Ski pass sales are typically handled at dedicated counters in hotels or Bergbahn offices. Staff may refer to "Bergbahn-Ticketschalter" or hotel concierge.

likely_response.local: Fragen Sie am Schalter nach Skipässen.
likely_response.translation_en: Ask at the counter for ski passes.

responses.english: Ask at the counter for ski passes.
responses.local: Fragen Sie am Schalter nach Skipässen.

phrases.intent_en: Is the Kulm Hotel building historic
phrases.cultural_note: The Kulm Hotel is a landmark in St. Moritz, known for its historic significance dating back to the 19th century. Staff may confirm its heritage status.

phrases.intent_en: Where is the Signalbahn funicular base
phrases.cultural_note: In Swiss mountain resorts, funicular stations are often well-signposted with distinctive brown signs. Staff may refer to "Talstation" or "Bergstation" for valley/mountain stations.

likely_response.translation_en: It is down by the lake.

responses.english: It is down by the lake.

phrases.intent_en: Which way to Via Serlas luxury street
phrases.cultural_note: Via Serlas is St. Moritz’s premier luxury shopping street. Signs will typically indicate "Fussgängerzone" or "Via Serlas". Staff may refer to it as the shopping promenade.
